paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And when Balaam saw that it pleased Jehovah to bless Israel, he went not, as at the other times, to meet with enchantments, but he set his face toward the wilderness.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Balaam lifted up his eyes, and he saw Israel dwelling according to their tribes; and the Spirit of God came upon him.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor saith, And the man whose eye was closed saith;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: He saith, who heareth the words of God, Who seeth the vision of the Almighty, Falling down, and having his eyes open:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: How goodly are thy tents, O Jacob, Thy tabernacles, O Israel!

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: As valleys are they spread forth, As gardens by the river-side, As lign-aloes which Jehovah hath planted, As cedar-trees beside the waters.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Water shall flow from his buckets, And his seed shall be in many waters, And his king shall be higher than Agag, And his kingdom shall be exalted.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: God bringeth him forth out of Egypt; He hath as it were the strength of the wild-ox: He shall eat up the nations his adversaries, And shall break their bones in pieces, And smite [them] through with his arrows.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: He couched, he lay down as a lion, And as a lioness; who shall rouse him up? Blessed be every one that blesseth thee, And cursed be every one that curseth thee.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Balak`s anger was kindled against Balaam, and he smote his hands together; and Balak said unto Balaam, I called thee to curse mine enemies, and, behold, thou hast altogether blessed them these three times.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Therefore now flee thou to thy place: I thought to promote thee unto great honor; but, lo, Jehovah hath kept thee back from honor.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Balaam said unto Balak, Spake I not also to thy messengers that thou sentest unto me, saying,

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the word of Jehovah, to do either good or bad of mine own mind; what Jehovah speaketh, that will I speak?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And now, behold, I go unto my people: come, [and] I will advertise thee what this people shall do to thy people in the latter days.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he took up his parable, and said, Balaam the son of Beor saith, And the man whose eye was closed saith;

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: He saith, who heareth the words of God, And knoweth the knowledge of the Most High, Who seeth the vision of the Almighty, Falling down, and having his eyes open:

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: I see him, but not now; I behold him, but not nigh: There shall come forth a star out of Jacob, And a sceptre shall rise out of Israel, And shall smite through the corners of Moab, And break down all the sons of tumult.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Edom shall be a possession, Seir also shall be a possession, [who were] his enemies; While Israel doeth valiantly.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And out of Jacob shall one have dominion, And shall destroy the remnant from the city.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he looked on Amalek, and took up his parable, and said, Amalek was the first of the nations; But his latter end shall come to destruction.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he looked on the Kenite, and took up his parable, and said, Strong is thy dwelling-place, And thy nest is set in the rock.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: Nevertheless Kain shall be wasted, Until Asshur shall carry thee away captive.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And he took up his parable, and said, Alas, who shall live when God doeth this?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: But ships [shall come] from the coast of Kittim, And they shall afflict Asshur, and shall afflict Eber; And he also shall come to destruction.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.text: And Balaam rose up, and went and returned to his place; and Balak also went his way.
